headline: SME Loan Rejection Rate Climbs to 52% at Major Banks
context: Bank Negara data showed that 52% of SME loan applications were rejected by commercial banks in 2025, up from 41% in 2022, with micro-enterprises and Bumiputera-owned firms facing the highest barriers.

cards.big: Every second SME loan application gets rejected — 52% failure rate is choking Malaysia's 1.2 million small businesses
cards.sub: Banks demand 2 years of audited accounts but 78% of micro-SMEs can't afford annual audits

cards.big: SMEs contribute 38.4% of GDP and employ 7.3 million workers — but receive only 18% of total bank lending
cards.sub: Credit guarantee schemes like CGC covered only RM12 billion versus RM48 billion SME financing gap

cards.big: Bumiputera SME loan rejection rate at 61% versus 44% for non-Bumiputera — despite equity policy goals
cards.sub: Collateral requirements averaging 120% of loan value exclude asset-poor entrepreneurs

cards.big: Bank Negara's SME financing target of 22% of total lending missed for 6 consecutive years since 2019
cards.sub: No enforcement mechanism for financing targets — banks face zero consequences for non-compliance

cards.big: Banks claim they support SMEs in glossy CSR reports — the rejection data tells a completely different story
Should Bank Negara impose mandatory SME lending quotas with penalties for banks that fall below 20% threshold?
